response:
Yes, it is possible to run a different `create_app` function when executing CLI commands compared to running the Flask server. This can be achieved by leveraging Flask’s CLI capabilities and customizing the app creation process based on the context in which the CLI command is executed. When using the app factory pattern, the `create_app` function is typically called once when the app is initialized. However, when running a CLI command, you can pass parameters or use context clues to determine whether to use a different configuration or even a different `create_app` function.

One approach is to use the `flask` command-line interface and pass arguments to the CLI command using the `@app.cli.command` decorator. You can define a CLI command that accepts parameters, which can be used to influence how the app is created. For example, you can define a CLI command that runs with a specific configuration or behavior based on the provided arguments. This allows you to avoid the need for environment variables by using command-line parameters directly.

Another method is to use a custom CLI command that checks the context in which it is executed. For instance, you can determine whether the app is being run as a server or as a CLI command by inspecting the current context. This can be done by checking the presence of certain flags or by using the `flask` command-line interface’s built-in capabilities. This approach allows you to avoid setting environment variables altogether.

You can also use the `flask` CLI to pass parameters directly to your custom commands. For example, when running `flask my_command x`, the `x` can be used to trigger a specific behavior. This way, you don't need to set an environment variable, and the command line becomes more intuitive and user-friendly.

It’s important to note that the `create_app` function is typically called once when the app is initialized. However, when running a CLI command, you can pass arguments that influence how the app is created. This can be done by defining a custom CLI command that modifies the configuration or behavior of the app before it is initialized.

If you want to run a different `create_app` function for CLI commands, you can define a separate function that creates the app with a different configuration. This function can be called within the CLI command instead of the default `create_app`. This allows you to have multiple versions of the app based on the context in which it is used.

You can also use the `flask` CLI to pass parameters to your commands using the `@app.cli.command
